reasoning: Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict stems from a false positive in cargo-vendor-archive.sh. The script's eval usage with a hardcoded CARGO variable and trusted upstream source context poses no practical risk. All other findings align with standard build processes.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen overflagged cargo-vendor-archive.sh's eval usage as command injection, while DeepSeek correctly recognized it as a controlled build helper in trusted context.
false_positives: cargo-vendor-archive.sh: eval usage in trusted build context flagged as command injection
learnings: Improve handling of eval in build scripts - distinguish between trusted upstream usage vs untrusted input contexts
Prioritize review of scripts actually executed during makepkg (build/package phases) over contrib/ helpers
Enhance model understanding of standard VLC build patterns to reduce false positives
